text.title: growth-referral-programs Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-referral-programs as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Fraud explosion: self-referrals, referral farms, multi-account abuse without deterministic detection rules.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents detect patterns but final ban decisions need human-in-loop. Deterministic feature computation (IP overlap, payment-method overlap, signup velocity) paired with rules-engine flags allows human review without LLM hallucination.

failure-mode.description: Mis-attribution when users clear cookies, switch devices, or use ad blockers — first-touch vs last-touch logic not explicit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Attribution ambiguity must be resolved in writing before launch. Default to last-touch for simplicity unless you have cross-device tracking infrastructure; document the choice in every dashboard.

failure-mode.description: Reward fulfillment lag (manual credit approval) breaks the trust loop.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Requires idempotent automation. Use a unique (referrer_id, referee_id, event) key to make credit operations idempotent and reduce manual review to fraud exceptions only.

failure-mode.description: Viral coefficient (K) plateaus quickly; agents over-optimizing reward size erode margin without meaningful K lift.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: K improves with friction reduction (invites/user = i) and conversion rate (c). Reward size affects only motivation; above-optimal rewards are pure expense. Focus agent optimization on cycle-time reduction and friction cuts first.
